rulings.Case Title: ELLEN LEE ZHOU VS. HILLARY KUNINS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismissal Orders
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 17, 2024, Line 1. 1 - PETITIONER ELLEN ZHOU's Motion To Dismissal Orders. Denied. The City's Opposition avoids one of the points of this motion which is that the City agreed to re-set the hearing from February 14 to March 28, 2024. The emails appended to the motion show the City did agree to March 28. But plaintiff did nothing to continue the February 14 date to any other date including to the March 28 date. This is despite a communication from the City's attorney, dated January 31, 2024 at 04:41:31 PM PST, that it would be the plaintiff who would "have to arrange with the Court" a new date. Plaintiff does not comply with CCP sec. 473b's requirement of appending the proposed filing; nor does the plaintiff offer the Opposition she suggests she inadvertently failed to file in connection with the underlying motion. Plaintiff filed no opposition to the underlying motion and did not contest the tentative. This may have been a function of her belief that the motion was not to be heard in February; but to secure relief now there has to be at least some showing that her failure to move the hearing to March 28, or otherwise contact the court to do something about the February 14 hearing date, was the result of excusable neglect. The current motion does not do so. It does not explain what the neglect was, nor why it was excusable. Under these circumstances, the motion is denied. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/CK)

For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/CK)
